expectations.evidence: Phase 1 'Automated Testing' includes commands for axe-core ('npx axe http://localhost:3000 --rules wcag2aa,wcag22aa') and Lighthouse ('npx lighthouse http://localhost:3000 --only-categories=accessibility'). Notes automated tools catch ~30-40% of issues.

expectations.text: Response includes manual testing phase (keyboard, screen reader)
expectations.evidence: Phase 3 'Manual Keyboard Testing' covers tabbing, reverse tab, activation, carousel controls, escape, skip nav, focus traps, and focus visibility with a detailed table. Phase 4 'Screen Reader Testing' covers VoiceOver testing for landmarks, headings, carousel, forms, and dynamic content.

Remediation Plan has four phases: 'Phase 1: Critical Fixes (Days 1-2)', 'Phase 2: High Priority (Days 3-4)', 'Phase 3: Medium Priority (Days 5-6)', 'Phase 4: Low Priority (Post-Launch Backlog)'. Each lists issues with priority numbers, fixes, effort estimates, and WCAG SC references.

expectations.text: Response references specific WCAG success criteria
Response produces a structured audit report
expectations.evidence: Report follows a formal structure: Executive Summary, Five-Phase Methodology (automated, semi-automated, keyboard, screen reader, visual), Section-by-Section Checklist (Hero, Feature Cards, Testimonial Carousel, Pricing Table, Contact Form, Page-Level), Scoring Framework with weighted categories, Remediation Plan phased by severity, and Verification Checklist.
